name: George Duvivier
abstract: George Duvivier (August 17, 1920 – July 11, 1985) was an American jazz double-bassist.

about_quotes.speaker: Eddie Metz Jr.
about_quotes.evidence: I got to play with George Duvivier. I got to play with all those people we saw at least one time. George Duvivier I played two sets with him.
about_quotes.doc_title: Eddie Metz Jr. interviewed by Monk Rowe, New Hartford, New York, October 3, 2015

about_quotes.speaker: Nellie Lutcher
about_quotes.evidence: I had -- when I first went I took Earl Hyde on drums, and George Duvivier... They went with me the first time, and the second time... George Duvivier went to Europe with me.

about_quotes.speaker: Bob Rosengarden
about_quotes.evidence: And I hired two bass players, Milt Hinton and George Duvivier. And I said to them together... I don’t care who shows up, one of you guys has to be there every day.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bob Rosengarden interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, October 6, 1996

about_quotes.speaker: Kristen Korb
about_quotes.evidence: listening to everything that I could get my hands on. I mean that was most of it. ... Leroy Vinnegar, and George Duvivier, Major Holly, Slam Stewart
about_quotes.doc_title: Kristen Korb interviewed by Monk Rowe, Scottsdale, Arizona, April 16, 2000

about_quotes.speaker: Chico Hamilton
about_quotes.evidence: Dick had given me a shot with my trio, with George Duvivier, and that’s when I made the trio record... George Duvivier was a monster.
about_quotes.doc_title: Chico Hamilton — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Urbie Green
about_quotes.evidence: So Benny took me and Stan Getz and Buck Clayton and George Duvivier and Gene Krupa, Buck Clayton, out to do the soundtrack.
about_quotes.doc_title: Urbie Green interviewed by Monk Rowe and Michael Woods, Majesty of the Seas, May 29, 1995
